trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Malesia exports electronics, palm oil, and rubber; Svezia exports telecom equipment (Ericsson), vehicles, machinery, and pharmaceuticals. Ericsson has significant operations in Malesia as a telecom equipment hub. IKEA sources some products from Malesian suppliers.

diplomatic.summary: Normal bilateral relations. Svezia-Malesia relations are conducted partly through EU-ASEAN frameworks. Svezia has raised human rights concerns about Malesia's treatment of opposition politicians and migrant workers through multilateral channels. Ericsson's major Malesian operations provide a strong economic foundation.

military.summary: No significant bilateral military relationship. Svezia's defense exports (Gripen fighter has been marketed to Malesia) have not resulted in major deals.

financial.summary: Ericsson and other Swedish companies are significant direct investors in Malesia. Svezia's AP funds hold Malesian equities. Malesian investment in Svezia is minimal.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Svezia imports no Malesian oil directly. Some collaboration on renewable energy technology.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation especially in ICT, environment, and health sciences. KTH and Chalmers collaborate with Malesian technical universities. Ericsson sponsors research programs at Malesian institutions.
